overview.title: YM TestCase User Manual
overview.info1: YM TestCase is a web-based test management system for managing test cases (TC), executing tests, analyzing results, and generating reports.
overview.li1: Project-level test case management (CRUD, Excel Import/Export)
overview.li2: Test execution and result recording (keyboard shortcuts, file attachments)
overview.li4: Dashboard (donut charts, bar charts, heatmap analysis)
overview.li6: Role-based access control (Admin, QA Manager, User)

login.title: 1. Login / Registration
login.li4: Upon success, you will be redirected to the project list page.
login.tip1: TIP: You can attempt up to 10 logins within 5 minutes on failure. Exceeding this will temporarily lock your account.
login.info1: Stay logged in: If you check the "Stay logged in" checkbox, your session will persist until the server restarts. Without it, you will be automatically logged out after 72 hours (3 days).
login.reg_li1: Click the "Sign Up" link at the bottom of the login screen.
login.reg_li2: Enter your username, display name, and password.
login.warn1: Password rules: Password must be at least 8 characters long.
login.info2: Note: The first registered user is automatically assigned the Admin role. Subsequent users start with the User role.
login.pw_li1: Select Change Password from the user info area after logging in.
login.info3: Forced change: If an administrator has reset your password, you will be required to change it on your next login. You cannot use other features until the change is completed.

projectList.title: 2. Project List
projectList.p1: This is the first screen after login. You can get an overview of all projects at a glance.
projectList.feat_summary_desc: Displays total projects, total TCs, progress, and Pass Rate as cards.
projectList.feat_progress_desc: Visualizes PASS/FAIL/BLOCK/N/A/Not Executed ratios as a colored bar.
projectList.feat_table_desc: Shows TC count, PASS/FAIL count, progress, and Pass Rate per project in a table.
projectList.cards_li1: Each card displays the project name, description, TC count, Pass/Fail count, progress, and recent activity date.
projectList.cards_li2: Click a card to navigate to that project's detail page.
projectList.create_li1: Click the "+ New Project" button in the upper right.
projectList.create_li2: Enter the project name and description.
projectList.warn1: Permission: Only Admin role can create projects.
projectList.bulk_li1: Select projects using the checkboxes on the left side of the overview table.
projectList.bulk_li2: Click the header checkbox to select/deselect all.
projectList.bulk_li3: After selecting, click the "Delete N" button to confirm and bulk delete.

projectDetail.title: 3. Project Detail
projectDetail.p1: Enter by clicking a project card or using the header selector. Switch features using tabs.
projectDetail.tab_tc_desc: Test case CRUD, Excel Import/Export, change history
projectDetail.tab_testrun_desc: Create test runs, record results, attachments, shortcuts
projectDetail.tab_compare_desc: Compare results between two test runs, regression detection
projectDetail.tab_settings_desc: Edit project info, Jira integration, member management

tcManage.title: 4. TC Management
tcManage.row_addRow_desc: Instantly creates a new TC and adds it to the grid (auto-saved)
tcManage.row_multiAdd_desc: Select 1/3/5/10/20/30 rows from the dropdown to add multiple TCs at once
tcManage.row_clone_desc: Clones selected TCs on the server as new TCs. TC ID gets a -copy suffix. Multiple TCs can be bulk cloned at once
tcManage.row_delete_desc: Soft deletes selected TCs. Deleted TCs can be restored within 7 days; after 7 days they are permanently deleted
tcManage.row_undo_desc: Undo grid edits (Ctrl+Z) or redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z). Supports up to 200 steps. History resets on page navigation
tcManage.row_autoId_desc: Auto-fills empty TC IDs in TC-001, TC-002... format. Sequential numbering starts after the highest existing ID
tcManage.row_bulkEdit_desc: Bulk modify specific fields of selected TCs
tcManage.row_import_desc: Import TCs from Excel files. Multiple sheets can be selected. Duplicate TC IDs are overwritten
tcManage.row_export_desc: Download current TC list as a formatted Excel file
tcManage.row_history_desc: Select 1 TC and click to view all test execution results as a timeline by run. Shows each run's result (PASS/FAIL/BLOCK etc.), version, round, and execution date
tcManage.row_changelog_desc: Opens the change log panel showing field-level before/after values, who made the change, and when, in chronological order. View project-wide or TC-specific history
tcManage.row_replace_desc: Enter search and replace text → select target field → click "Replace" for bulk replacement. Can target selected rows only or all rows
tcManage.row_search_desc: Filter by TC ID, title, category, etc.
tcManage.folderSheet_p1: You can organize TCs into folders and sheets hierarchically within a project. Managed via a VS Code-style tree in the left sidebar.
tcManage.info_folder: 📁 Folder — Does not contain TCs directly; used to group sub-folders and sheets. Click to expand/collapse only
tcManage.info_sheet: 📄 Sheet — The unit where TCs are actually stored. Click to display the sheet's TCs in the grid
tcManage.fs_li1: In an empty project, "Add Folder", "Add Sheet", and "Import" buttons are displayed in the center of the screen.
tcManage.fs_li2: Add Folder: Click the 📁+ button at the top of the sidebar to add a root folder. Click the 📁+ button on a folder node to add a sub-folder.
tcManage.fs_li3: Add Sheet: Click the 📄+ button at the top of the sidebar to add a root sheet. Click the 📄+ button on a folder node to add a sheet inside the folder.
tcManage.fs_li4: Switch Sheet: Click a sheet (📄) in the sidebar to display only that sheet's TCs.
tcManage.fs_li5: View All: When there are 2 or more sheets, an "All" item appears at the top of the sidebar to view all sheets' TCs at once.
tcManage.fs_li6: Delete: Delete using the inline x button on folders/sheets. Deleting a folder also deletes all child items and TCs.
tcManage.fs_li7: On Import, Excel file sheet names are directly created as sheets in the sidebar tree.
tcManage.fs_li8: Sidebar collapse/expand: Use the ◀/▶ toggle button to collapse or expand the sidebar.
tcManage.grid_li1: Double-click a cell to enter inline edit mode.
tcManage.grid_li2: Use checkboxes to select rows for copy/delete/bulk edit operations.
tcManage.grid_li4: Cell edits are auto-saved. There is no separate save button.
tcManage.grid_li5: New rows are also saved to the server immediately and can be undone with Undo (Ctrl+Z).
tcManage.grid_li6: Drag and drop sorting: Grab the drag handle (≡) in the No column and drag rows up/down to reorder TCs. The new order is automatically saved to the server. (Edit permission required)
tcManage.dr_li1: When you delete a TC, it is soft deleted. It is not permanently deleted immediately.
tcManage.dr_li2: Deleted TCs can be restored within 7 days. After 7 days, they are automatically permanently deleted.
tcManage.dr_li3: If restoration is needed, contact an administrator or use the API: POST /api/projects/:id/testcases/:tc_id/restore
tcManage.warn1: Warning: When deleting a sheet, all TCs belonging to it are also deleted. If there are child sheets, their TCs are deleted as well.
tcManage.field_type_ex: Func., UI/UX, Perf., Security, API, Data
tcManage.field_priority_ex: Critical, High, Medium, Low, Very Low
tcManage.field_platform_ex: WEB, Mobile, iOS, Android, API, Common
tcManage.field_issueLink: Jira issue key. Enter the Jira Base URL in the Settings tab, then clicking will navigate to the issue page (e.g., https://jira.example.com/browse/PROJ-123)

sheetTree.title: 5. Folder / Sheet Tree Structure
sheetTree.p1: Manage TCs hierarchically with folders (📁) and sheets (📄). Displayed as a tree in the VS Code-style left sidebar.
sheetTree.sheet_role: Unit where TCs are actually stored
sheetTree.sb_li1: The sidebar is displayed on the left side of the screen, with folders and sheets listed in a tree format.
sheetTree.sb_li3: Sheets are shown with the 📄 icon, with the TC count displayed next to them.
sheetTree.sb_li4: Child items are visually distinguished by indentation (depth).
sheetTree.sb_li5: Use the ◀/▶ toggle button at the top of the sidebar to collapse or expand it.
sheetTree.add_li1: Add to root: Click the 📁+ (add folder) or 📄+ (add sheet) button at the top of the sidebar.
sheetTree.add_li2: Add inside folder: Click the 📁+ (sub-folder) or 📄+ (sub-sheet) button on a folder node.
sheetTree.add_li3: Child items cannot be added to sheet (📄) nodes.
sheetTree.add_li4: The input form shows the type to add (📁 Folder / 📄 Sheet). Enter a name and press Enter or click the "Add" button.
sheetTree.del_li1: Deleting a folder also deletes all sub-folders, sheets, and TCs.
sheetTree.del_li2: The deletion confirmation message shows the number of child items.
sheetTree.del_li3: Delete using the inline x button on folders/sheets.
sheetTree.info1: Backward compatible: If you don't use folders, you can manage TCs with sheets only, just like before.

import.title: 6. Import (Excel / CSV / Markdown)
import.p1: In addition to Excel, you can also import TCs from CSV files. CSV files exported from Jira, Xray, and Zephyr Scale can be imported directly.
import.usage_li1: Click the Import button in the TC management toolbar.
import.usage_li2: Select a .csv, .xlsx, or .md file in the file selection dialog.
import.usage_li3: For CSV files, headers are automatically analyzed and mapped to TC fields.
import.enc_li1: UTF-8, UTF-8 BOM, and CP949 (EUC-KR) encodings are auto-detected.
import.enc_li2: Korean headers (대분류, 우선순위, 테스트 절차, etc.) are also auto-mapped.
import.enc_li3: Overwrite policy: If a TC ID in the import file matches an existing TC, all fields of that TC are updated with the file contents. Rows without a TC ID are added as new TCs.
import.tip1: TIP: Export issues from Jira as CSV: Issue search results → upper right "Export" → "CSV (All fields)"

mdImport.title: 6-1. Markdown Import
mdImport.p1: You can import tables written in Markdown (.md) files as TCs. Useful for document-based TC management or importing TCs written in GitHub/Wiki.
mdImport.usage_li1: Click the Import button in the TC management toolbar.
mdImport.usage_li2: Select a .md file in the file selection dialog.
mdImport.usage_li3: Markdown tables in the file are automatically parsed.
mdImport.usage_li4: If there are multiple tables, a sheet selection modal is displayed.
mdImport.format_p1: Uses standard Markdown table syntax. The first row is the header, the second row is the separator (|---|), and the rest is data.
mdImport.format_example:
# Login Test

| TC ID  | Category | Priority | Test Steps           | Expected Result      |
|--------|----------|----------|----------------------|----------------------|
| TC-001 | Login    | High     | Attempt normal login | Navigate to main     |
| TC-002 | Login    | Medium   | Enter wrong password | Show error message   |

## Search Feature

| TC ID  | Category | Priority | Test Steps           | Expected Result      |
|--------|----------|----------|----------------------|----------------------|
| TC-003 | Search   | High     | Search by keyword    | Display results      |
mdImport.sn_li1: The # or ## heading above a table becomes the sheet name.
mdImport.sn_li2: In the example above, "Login Test" and "Search Feature" become the respective sheet names.
mdImport.sn_li3: Tables without a heading use "MD Import" as the default name.
mdImport.sn_li4: Multiple tables in a single .md file are imported as separate sheets.
mdImport.hm_p1: Uses the same header mapping rules as Excel/CSV Import. Both Korean and English headers are supported.
mdImport.notes_li1: To use a pipe (|) inside a cell, escape it with \|.
mdImport.notes_li2: If a TC ID matches an existing TC, that TC is overwritten.
mdImport.notes_li3: Rows without a TC ID are auto-generated in MD-0001, MD-0002 format.
mdImport.notes_li4: Plain text (descriptions, lists, etc.) between tables is ignored.
mdImport.notes_li5: Encoding: UTF-8, UTF-8 BOM, CP949 (EUC-KR) auto-detected.
mdImport.tip1: TIP: You can save Markdown tables from GitHub, Notion, Obsidian, etc. as .md files and import them directly.

advancedFilter.title: 7. Advanced Filter + Saved Views
advancedFilter.p1: Filter TCs with multiple conditions and save frequently used filters for reuse.
advancedFilter.open_li1: Click the Filter button on the right side of the TC management toolbar.
advancedFilter.open_li2: The filter panel appears above the grid.
advancedFilter.open_li3: Click the + Add Condition button to add filter conditions.
advancedFilter.cond_field_desc: Select from TC ID, Type, Category, Priority, Assignee, etc.
advancedFilter.cond_operator_desc: Contains, Does not contain, Equals, Not equals, Is empty, Is not empty
advancedFilter.cond_value_desc: Enter filter value (not needed for Is empty/Is not empty)
advancedFilter.cond_logic_desc: AND (all match) or OR (any match). A single logical operator applies to all conditions (e.g., 3 conditions + AND → only TCs matching all 3 are shown)
advancedFilter.sl_li1: Save button: Save current filter conditions with a name.
advancedFilter.sl_li2: Load button: Select from saved filter list to restore.
advancedFilter.sl_li3: Reset button: Remove all filter conditions and show all TCs.
advancedFilter.tip1: TIP: When a filter is active, the filter button shows the condition count (e.g., Filter (3)).

testrun.title: 8. Test Execution
testrun.create_li1: Click the "+ New Test Run" button at the bottom of the left panel.
testrun.create_li2: Enter the run name, round (R1/R2/...), version, and environment.
testrun.create_li3: When created, all TCs in the project are included with NS (Not Started) status.
testrun.list_li1: All test runs for the project are displayed in the left panel.
testrun.list_li2: Run name, round, version, and status (In Progress/Completed) are shown.
testrun.list_li3: Click a run to display the results grid on the right.
testrun.feat_mouse_desc: Click a result cell to select PASS/FAIL/BLOCK/N/A/NS from the dropdown.
testrun.feat_keyboard_desc: After selecting a cell, press P (Pass), F (Fail), B (Block), N (NS) to quickly enter results.
testrun.feat_fill_desc: Use Ctrl+D to copy the current value downward, or Shift+Click to select a range.
testrun.feat_undo_desc: Ctrl+Z to undo, Ctrl+Shift+Z to redo. (Up to 200 steps)
testrun.att_li1: You can attach image/document files to result rows.
testrun.att_li2: Supported formats: Images (PNG, JPEG, GIF, BMP, WebP), Documents (PDF, DOC, XLSX, PPTX), Archives (ZIP), Text (TXT, CSV, LOG)
testrun.att_li4: Image files have a preview available.
testrun.rm_complete_desc: Changes the run status to "Completed". A confirmation message appears if there are unexecuted (NS) TCs remaining. Completed runs are locked from editing.
testrun.rm_reopen_desc: Reverts a completed run to "In Progress". Existing result data is preserved and not reset. Use when additional execution or result modifications are needed.
testrun.rm_clone_desc: Clones the run structure (name, round, environment, TC list) to create a new run. All results are reset to NS; attachments and comments are not cloned.
testrun.rm_delete_desc: Permanently deletes the run and all results and attachments. This cannot be undone, so use with caution.
testrun.rm_export_desc: Download run results as an Excel (.xlsx) file. Includes all fields: TC ID, title, result, comments, etc.
testrun.timer_li1: Turn on the timer toggle at the top of the run results grid to display a timer for each TC row.
testrun.timer_li2: The timer starts automatically when TC execution begins, and elapsed time is recorded when results are entered.
testrun.timer_li3: Recorded time is included in reports and exports.
testrun.tip1: TIP: The timer is optional. Turn off the toggle to enter results without time recording.

testPlans.title: 9. Test Plans / Milestones
testPlans.p1: Plan tests and manage progress on a per-release basis.
testPlans.create_li1: Create a test plan via the API: POST /api/projects/:id/testplans
testPlans.create_li2: Plans can have a name, milestone, description, start date, and end date.
testPlans.link_li1: Specify test_plan_id when creating a TestRun to link it to a plan.
testPlans.link_li2: Multiple TestRuns can be linked to a single plan.
testPlans.progress_li1: When viewing a plan, results from all linked TestRuns are aggregated and pass_rate is calculated.
testPlans.progress_li2: View total TC count, PASS/FAIL/BLOCK/NA/NS counts at a glance.
testPlans.info1: Note: When deleting a plan, linked TestRuns are not deleted; the Run's test_plan_id is set to null.

compare.title: 10. Compare
compare.p1: Select two test runs to compare results side by side.
compare.li1: Select the left (baseline) run and right (target) run.
compare.li2: In the comparison table, check the left/right results for each TC.
compare.li3: Use filters to view changed items only or regressions only (PASS→FAIL).
compare.info1: Statistics: The top shows total TC count, changed count, regression count, and improvement count.

dashboard.title: 11. Dashboard
dashboard.p1: Analyze selected test run results with various charts and statistics.
dashboard.sum_li1: Total TC: Total test case count and execution rate
dashboard.sum_li6: Not Executed: Not executed count and ratio
dashboard.chart_donut_desc: Visualizes PASS/FAIL/BLOCK/N/A/Not Executed ratios as a donut chart
dashboard.chart_bar_desc: Shows result trends by round (R1, R2, R3...) specified at test run creation as stacked bar chart. Multiple runs in the same round are aggregated
dashboard.chart_priority_desc: TC count by priority, PASS/FAIL/BLOCK/N/A/Not Executed counts table
dashboard.chart_heatmap_desc: Shows failure counts in a Category x Priority matrix using color intensity
dashboard.df_p1: Specify a date range at the top of the dashboard to analyze test results for a specific period.
dashboard.df_li1: Preset buttons: Click "All", "7 days", "30 days", "90 days" buttons to instantly filter data for that period.
dashboard.df_li2: Custom range: Manually enter start and end dates to specify your desired period.
dashboard.df_li3: The date filter applies to all charts and statistics (summary, priority, category, round, assignee, heatmap).
dashboard.df_li4: Can be used together with the run selection dropdown.

report.title: 12. Report
report.p1: View test run results as a summary report and download as PDF/Excel.
report.li1: Select a test run in the Report tab.
report.li2: Summary section: Check total TCs, counts/ratios by result, etc.
report.li3: Failure list: View TC details for FAIL results.
report.li4: PDF download: Click the PDF button to download a formatted PDF report.
report.li5: Excel download: Click the Excel button to download an Excel report.

settings.title: 13. Settings
settings.pi_li1: You can edit the project name and description.
settings.pi_li2: Jira Base URL: Enter the Jira server address (e.g., https://jira.example.com). Once set, clicking an issue key (e.g., PROJ-123) in the TC's Issue Link field navigates to Jira Base URL/browse/PROJ-123.
settings.pi_li3: Public/Private: Public projects are viewable by all logged-in users (edits require membership). Private projects are accessible only to project members and the creator. Default is Public.
settings.df_p1: You can rename display names and hide default TC fields (TC ID, Type, Category, etc.) for the project.
settings.df_li1: Display name: Change default English headers to localized or team-specific terms (e.g., Category → Module, Depth 1 → Major Category).
settings.df_li2: Display checkbox: Hide unused fields from the grid.
settings.df_li3: Required fields: TC ID, Test Steps, and Expected Result cannot be hidden.
settings.df_li4: Click Save to apply. Use the Reset button to restore defaults.
settings.df_li5: Settings are managed independently per project. Import/Export uses internal field names (English), so there is no impact.
settings.cf_p1: You can add user-defined columns to TCs per project.
settings.cf_text: Free text input. Edit by double-clicking a cell
settings.cf_number: Numbers only. Non-numeric input is ignored
settings.cf_select: Single-select dropdown. Choose one from options defined in settings
settings.cf_checkbox: Checkbox toggle. Click to switch true/false
settings.cf_date: Date picker (calendar popup) for date input
settings.cf_mgmt_li1: Manage custom fields in the project Settings tab.
settings.cf_mgmt_li2: Add field: Select the field name and type (6 types), and for select/multiselect types, enter options to add.
settings.cf_mgmt_li3: Added fields appear in the list table and can be removed with the Delete button.
settings.cf_mgmt_li4: Created fields automatically add columns to the TC management grid.
settings.cf_mgmt_li5: Select types automatically apply dropdown editors.
settings.tip1: TIP: Custom field values are stored in the TC's custom_fields JSON field. They are preserved during Import/Export.
settings.mem_li1: Add users to the project and assign project roles.
settings.mem_li2: Project roles: Project Admin (project management), Project Tester (test execution)
settings.mem_li3: Role changes and member removal are available (project creator cannot be removed).

roles.title: 14. Role-based Permissions
roles.access_public_desc: All authenticated users can view (editing depends on role)
roles.access_private_desc: Only project members or creators can access
roles.access_admin_desc: System Admin / QA Manager can access all projects
roles.h3_dualRoles: 14-3. Relationship Between System Roles and Project Roles
roles.info1: Dual role structure: System roles (Admin/QA Manager/User) determine global permissions, while project roles (Project Admin/Project Tester) determine permissions within that project.
roles.dual_li1: System Admin: Full access to all projects. All features available regardless of project role
roles.dual_li2: QA Manager: Can access all projects. Can create projects. Admin-level permissions within projects
roles.dual_li3: User + Project Admin: Can create/edit/delete TCs, manage runs, and change settings in that project
roles.dual_li4: User + Project Tester: Can record test results, view dashboard/reports in that project. Cannot create/delete TCs
roles.dual_li5: User (not a member): Can only view public projects. Cannot access private projects

header.title: 15. Header / Navigation
header.row_logo_desc: Click to navigate to the project list page
header.row_selector_desc: Select a project from the dropdown to immediately navigate to that project's detail page
header.row_search_desc: Enter 2+ characters to search TCs across all projects. Click a result to navigate to and highlight that TC
header.row_notification_desc: Notifications are automatically generated when a test run is completed. The bell icon shows unread notification count as a red badge. Click to open the notification list. Clicking a notification navigates to the test run and marks it as read. Use "Mark all read" to clear all. Runs with FAILs show the FAIL count.
header.row_theme_desc: Click the 🌙 icon to switch themes. Setting is saved in browser
header.row_admin_desc: Shown only for Admin role. Navigates to user management page
header.row_user_desc: Shows the currently logged-in username and role badge
header.row_logout_desc: Click to logout and return to the login page

theme.title: 16. Theme (Dark Mode)
theme.p1: Click the 🌙 icon in the header to switch between light and dark themes.
theme.li1: Theme settings are saved to browser local storage and persist across sessions.
theme.li2: The theme is applied uniformly across all pages, grids, and charts.
